paper_title: Economic Growth and Complexity in the UAE: Summary Report
research_answer: Re-exports account for 40% of the UAE's total goods exports, making them a substantial pillar of its trade profile. The major product categories in this re-export flow are electronics, vehicles, and textiles. It's also notable that 40% of the country's exports — including these re-exports — flow through the UAE's free zones, highlighting the role of those zones as logistics and trade facilitation hubs. When re-exports are stripped out, non-oil domestically produced goods exports grew at just over 5% per year between 2005 and 2019 — meaningfully slower than the 7.7% headline non-oil goods figure — and have been largely stagnant since 2011–2012.
supporting_quotes: Re-exports make up 40% of the UAE's total goods exports, with a heavy representation of electronics, vehicles, and textiles.
40% of the country's exports (including re-exports) occur from the free zones.
Non-oil and re-export goods exports have grown a little more than 5% per year between 2005 and 2019.
Post 2011-2012, we can see that most non-oil goods sector exports have more or less stagnated.

data_points.metric: re-export share of total goods exports
share of exports from free zones
non-oil and re-export goods export growth rate
